sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, score FLOAT DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, score) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 95.0), (2, 'BOB', 85.5), (3, 'charLIE', NULL), (4, 'DAVID', 75.25);
SELECT id, lower(name) + score AS combined FROM test_table WHERE score IS NOT NULL ORDER BY combined;
SELECT id, lower(name) + (SELECT MAX(score) FROM test_table) AS total FROM test_table GROUP BY id, lower(name) HAVING total > 100;
SELECT A.id, lower(A.name) + lower(B.name) AS combined_name FROM test_table A JOIN test_table B ON A.id != B.id ORDER BY combined_name LIMIT 2;
SELECT id, lower(name) + SUM(CASE WHEN score IS NOT NULL THEN score ELSE 0 END) AS aggregate_score FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING aggregate_score < 100;
SELECT id, lower(name) + ' score: ' + CAST(score AS VARCHAR) AS detailed_info FROM test_table WHERE score + 5 > 90;
SELECT A.id, lower(A.name) + COALESCE(B.name, 'Unknown') AS complete_name FROM test_table A LEFT JOIN (SELECT id, NULL AS name FROM test_table) B ON A.id = B.id WHERE lower(A.name) <> '';
SELECT id, lower(name) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE score IS NULL) AS null_count FROM test_table;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, score) VALUES (5, NULL, NULL), (6, '  ', -20), (7, 'Eve', 100.00);
